title: Product boundary manifest
description: Classifies every product capability against the open-source core versus managed / commercial / service-plane boundary. Pairs with docs/product/boundary_manifest_strawman.md (narrative strawman) and, when the machine register lands, with artifacts/product/boundary_manifest.yaml (authoritative for tooling). The manifest does NOT encode pricing or product packaging; it only records the technical and product boundary and the residual-dependency surface each capability exposes when optional services are absent.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this document. Increment on breaking field changes.

deployment_profiles.description: Enumerated deployment profile ids. Every capability row's deployment_profiles entries MUST resolve to an id here. Profiles record the operational stance (who runs the control plane, whether sign-in is present, whether the environment has public internet) without prejudging pricing.

capabilities.description: Capability rows. One row per product capability in scope at the current milestone. Every row is explicitly classified against the open-source core boundary and records the residual dependencies, data boundary, portability story, and absence-narrowing posture for that capability.

id.description: Stable profile id. Referenced from capability rows' deployment_profiles arrays.

sign_in_expected.description: Whether this profile normally carries an authenticated identity session. The local-core continuity rule applies independently: capabilities classified local_core MUST remain usable when this is false.

public_internet_expected.description: Whether this profile normally has public internet egress. Air-gapped profiles set this to false.

control_plane_operator.description: Who runs the control plane for this profile: self, vendor, customer, or none. Free text so new operator models do not require a schema bump.

id.description: Stable capability id. Do not reuse when a capability is superseded; mint a new id and point superseded_by at it.

classification.description: Where the capability sits on the boundary. local_core: must work with no network, no sign-in, no hosted service. self_host_friendly: optional service whose protocol and reference implementation are designed for customer or third-party operation. managed_convenience: hosted-only value-add layered on top of a self-hostable protocol; the underlying workflow remains reachable via the self-hostable path. out_of_scope: explicitly not in the open-core or managed roadmap. No silent blanks.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of what the capability is, scoped narrowly enough that its boundary classification is unambiguous.

deployment_profiles.description: Deployment profile ids in which this capability is expected to be available. Every id MUST resolve to a deployment_profiles[].id above. A row reserves the deployment-profile surface even when it has no hosted instance yet.

local_core_continuity.description: Explicit statement of what still works locally when every optional service this row touches is absent. For local_core rows this restates the invariant. For other rows it names the fallback path (for example: 'editor continues with no marketplace; previously installed extensions keep loading from the local cache'). No silent blanks: managed_convenience rows MUST state the local-core behaviour that persists when the managed form is unavailable.

residual_dependencies.description: External systems, services, or credentials the capability still depends on in its normal form. Each entry is a placeholder slot the first implementation will fill; the slot is reserved here so later claim packets do not invent new fields.

absence_narrows_to.description: The narrowed claim the capability carries when sign-in is absent or the hosted control plane is unreachable. This field exists so absence narrows a claim rather than reclassifying the capability: for example, a collaboration session narrows to 'local edits continue; presence is unavailable' without becoming out-of-scope.

linked_decisions.description: Decision ids from artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml that gate or constrain this capability's boundary. Empty while no decision is on the register.

linked_lanes.description: Lane ids from artifacts/governance/ownership_matrix.yaml#scorecard_lane_index that own this capability's implementation. The manifest does not mint new lanes; it only points at existing ones.

source_anchors.description: Pointers into source documents that motivate the classification. Used so the row can be traced back without restating the spec.

status.description: Strawman lifecycle. Rows are proposed when first drafted; accepted when a decision or ADR ratifies the classification; deferred when the row cannot be closed at the target milestone; superseded when a later row replaces it.

superseded_by.description: Capability ids that supersede this row. Non-empty only when status is superseded.

allOf.description: superseded_by MUST be non-empty when status is superseded.
out_of_scope rows MUST NOT claim hosted or managed deployment profiles beyond the baseline individual_local profile, which is reserved so the row still resolves against the enumerated set.

description.description: Short description of the residual dependency. Implementation slots are reserved here even if the first implementation fills them later.

self_hostable.description: Whether the dependency has (or is planned to have) a self-hostable reference implementation or protocol.

absence_impact.description: What the user experiences when this dependency is unavailable. Pairs with the row's absence_narrows_to field.

primary_store.description: Where the capability's primary state lives: local disk, workspace repo, customer control plane, vendor control plane, none, or a named combination.

crosses_device_boundary.description: Whether normal use sends user data off the device it was authored on.

residency_controls.description: Region / residency controls available to the operator (for example, region pinning, customer-managed keys, or BYO storage).

export_safe.description: Whether the capability's state can be exported in an inspectable, redaction-aware form.

export_format.description: Format the user can export their state in. File-based and text-based formats are strongly preferred so state remains inspectable without the product.

offboarding.description: What offboarding looks like: how a user or organization leaves the capability without data loss. For managed_convenience rows this MUST describe the path back to the self-hostable or local form.

migration_story.description: How a user moves between deployment profiles (for example, from managed_cloud to self_hosted). Optional; populated when the capability actively supports migration.

anchor.description: Line number, heading slug, or other stable anchor inside the referenced document.
